title: erythroxylum excelsum
intro: Erythroxylum excelsum, also known as the sacred tree or mountain coca, is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America, belonging to the Erythroxylum genus. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and act as an anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of Erythroxylum excelsum include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve circulation, and provide antioxidant properties, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions. Erythroxylum excelsum is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional ceremonies and rituals in the Andean region.
